Sentiment, Topics, and Emotional Patterns in AI Startup Reviews

Salma Vikha Ainindita - DATA 407 Final Report

2026-04-06

Load Libraries

library(DBI)
library(RSQLite)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(scales)

Preprocessing

db_path <- "./dataset/reviews.sqlite"
doc_topics_path <- "./dataset/doc_topics_all_n30.csv"
topic_summaries_path <- "./dataset/openai_topic_summaries.csv"

con <- dbConnect(SQLite(), db_path)

reviews_df <- dbGetQuery(con, "
SELECT
    review_pk,
    review_id,
    startup_id,
    platform_id,
    title,
    content,
    rating_original,
    rating_scale_max,
    rating_norm01,
    review_date,
    language,
    country_code
FROM reviews
WHERE rating_original IS NOT NULL
")

vad_df <- dbGetQuery(con, "
SELECT
    review_pk,
    valence,
    arousal,
    low_confidence,
    coverage_review
FROM review_vad_dominant_polarity
")

startups_df <- dbGetQuery(con, "
SELECT
    startup_id,
    cluster
FROM startups
")

dbDisconnect(con)

doc_topics <- read_csv(doc_topics_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
topic_summaries <- read_csv(topic_summaries_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

topic_summaries_clean <- topic_summaries %>%
  mutate(
    clean_title = str_extract(summary, '(?<="title": ")[^"]+')
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    topic_title = ifelse(is.na(clean_title), paste("Topic", topic_id), clean_title)
  ) %>%
  select(topic_id, topic_title)

doc_topics_clean <- doc_topics %>%
  filter(topic_id != -1) %>%
  group_by(review_pk) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = probability, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

master_df <- reviews_df %>%
  left_join(vad_df, by = "review_pk") %>%
  left_join(doc_topics_clean, by = "review_pk") %>%
  left_join(topic_summaries_clean, by = "topic_id") %>%
  left_join(startups_df, by = "startup_id") %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment = case_when(
      rating_original < 3 ~ "negative",
      rating_original == 3 ~ "neutral",
      rating_original > 3 ~ "positive",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sentiment = factor(sentiment, levels = c("negative", "neutral", "positive")),
    sentiment_score = case_when(
      sentiment == "negative" ~ -1,
      sentiment == "neutral" ~ 0,
      sentiment == "positive" ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    is_positive = ifelse(sentiment == "positive", 1, 0),
    is_negative = ifelse(sentiment == "negative", 1, 0),
    is_neutral  = ifelse(sentiment == "neutral", 1, 0),
    extreme_rating = ifelse(rating_original %in% c(1, 5), 1, 0)
  )

cat("Rows in master dataset:", nrow(master_df), "\n")
## Rows in master dataset: 33169
cat("Rows with non-missing topic:", sum(!is.na(master_df$topic_title)), "\n")
## Rows with non-missing topic: 28234
cat("Rows with non-missing cluster:", sum(!is.na(master_df$cluster)), "\n")
## Rows with non-missing cluster: 33169
cat("Rows with non-missing arousal:", sum(!is.na(master_df$arousal)), "\n")
## Rows with non-missing arousal: 32599

Research Questions

  1. RQ1 (Sampling Bias in Reviews): Does the overall positivity of AI reviews change after adjusting for startup-cluster imbalance?
  2. RQ2 (Topic Distribution and Satisfaction): Do review topics vary across startup clusters, and are some topics associated with higher or lower satisfaction?
  3. RQ3 (Emotion Intensity vs Ratings): Are extreme ratings (such as 1-star and 5-star reviews) associated with different arousal levels than non-extreme ratings?

RQ1: Sampling Bias in Reviews

rq1_df <- master_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(sentiment), !is.na(cluster)) %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.factor(cluster))

cat("Rows used for RQ1:", nrow(rq1_df), "\n")
## Rows used for RQ1: 33169
cat("Clusters used:", n_distinct(rq1_df$cluster), "\n")
## Clusters used: 4
naive_sentiment <- rq1_df %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    positive_prop = mean(is_positive, na.rm = TRUE),
    negative_prop = mean(is_negative, na.rm = TRUE),
    neutral_prop  = mean(is_neutral, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print(naive_sentiment)
##       n positive_prop negative_prop neutral_prop
## 1 33169     0.6061986      0.334077   0.05972444
cluster_satisfaction <- rq1_df %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_satisfaction = mean(sentiment_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_satisfaction = sd(sentiment_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_satisfaction = sd_satisfaction / sqrt(n),
    positive_prop = mean(sentiment == "positive"),
    negative_prop = mean(sentiment == "negative"),
    neutral_prop = mean(sentiment == "neutral"),
    var_positive_h = var(is_positive, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(cluster_satisfaction)
## # A tibble: 4 × 9
##   cluster     n mean_satisfaction sd_satisfaction se_satisfaction positive_prop
##   <fct>   <int>             <dbl>           <dbl>           <dbl>         <dbl>
## 1 1       13050            0.0211           0.960         0.00841         0.472
## 2 2        9646            0.382            0.905         0.00921         0.673
## 3 3        1459            0.942            0.303         0.00794         0.960
## 4 4        9014            0.410            0.875         0.00922         0.672
## # ℹ 3 more variables: negative_prop <dbl>, neutral_prop <dbl>,
## #   var_positive_h <dbl>
H <- n_distinct(cluster_satisfaction$cluster)

cluster_satisfaction <- cluster_satisfaction %>%
  mutate(W_h_equal = 1 / H)

positive_stratified <- sum(cluster_satisfaction$W_h_equal * cluster_satisfaction$positive_prop, na.rm = TRUE)
negative_stratified <- sum(cluster_satisfaction$W_h_equal * cluster_satisfaction$negative_prop, na.rm = TRUE)
neutral_stratified  <- sum(cluster_satisfaction$W_h_equal * cluster_satisfaction$neutral_prop, na.rm = TRUE)

comparison_df <- tibble(
  estimate_type = c("naive", "stratified_equal_weight"),
  positive_prop = c(naive_sentiment$positive_prop, positive_stratified),
  negative_prop = c(naive_sentiment$negative_prop, negative_stratified),
  neutral_prop  = c(naive_sentiment$neutral_prop, neutral_stratified)
)

print(comparison_df)
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   estimate_type           positive_prop negative_prop neutral_prop
##   <chr>                           <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>
## 1 naive                           0.606         0.334       0.0597
## 2 stratified_equal_weight         0.694         0.256       0.0501
var_positive_stratified <- sum(
  (cluster_satisfaction$W_h_equal^2) *
    (cluster_satisfaction$var_positive_h / cluster_satisfaction$n),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

se_positive_stratified <- sqrt(var_positive_stratified)

stratified_precision <- tibble(
  estimate = "positive_prop",
  stratified_estimate = positive_stratified,
  stratified_se = se_positive_stratified,
  ci_lower = positive_stratified - 1.96 * se_positive_stratified,
  ci_upper = positive_stratified + 1.96 * se_positive_stratified
)

print(stratified_precision)
## # A tibble: 1 × 5
##   estimate      stratified_estimate stratified_se ci_lower ci_upper
##   <chr>                       <dbl>         <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 positive_prop               0.694       0.00241    0.690    0.699
bootstrap_compare <- function(data, B = 1000) {
  n_rows <- nrow(data)
  clusters <- unique(na.omit(data$cluster))
  H <- length(clusters)

  naive_vals <- numeric(B)
  strat_vals <- numeric(B)

  for (b in 1:B) {
    boot_data <- data %>% slice_sample(n = n_rows, replace = TRUE)

    naive_vals[b] <- mean(boot_data$is_positive, na.rm = TRUE)

    stratum_boot <- boot_data %>%
      filter(!is.na(cluster)) %>%
      group_by(cluster) %>%
      summarise(
        positive_h = mean(is_positive, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      )

    stratum_boot <- tibble(cluster = clusters) %>%
      left_join(stratum_boot, by = "cluster") %>%
      mutate(
        positive_h = ifelse(is.na(positive_h), 0, positive_h),
        W_h = 1 / H
      )

    strat_vals[b] <- sum(stratum_boot$W_h * stratum_boot$positive_h, na.rm = TRUE)
  }

  tibble(
    estimate_type = c("naive", "stratified_equal_weight"),
    mean_estimate = c(mean(naive_vals), mean(strat_vals)),
    ci_lower = c(quantile(naive_vals, 0.025), quantile(strat_vals, 0.025)),
    ci_upper = c(quantile(naive_vals, 0.975), quantile(strat_vals, 0.975))
  )
}

set.seed(123)
bootstrap_summary <- bootstrap_compare(rq1_df, B = 1000)
print(bootstrap_summary)
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   estimate_type           mean_estimate ci_lower ci_upper
##   <chr>                           <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 naive                           0.606    0.601    0.612
## 2 stratified_equal_weight         0.694    0.690    0.699
ggplot(
  comparison_df,
  aes(x = estimate_type, y = positive_prop)
) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Naive vs Stratified Positive Review Estimate",
    x = "Estimator",
    y = "Positive Proportion"
  )

cluster_sentiment_counts <- rq1_df %>%
  count(cluster, sentiment) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(
  cluster_sentiment_counts,
  aes(x = cluster, y = prop, fill = sentiment)
) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Sentiment Composition by Startup Cluster",
    x = "Startup Cluster",
    y = "Proportion",
    fill = "Sentiment"
  )

Conclusion

The naive estimate suggests that 60.6% of reviews are positive, while the equal-weight stratified estimate suggests 69.4%. The difference is 8.8%. This shows that the overall positivity estimate changes depending on how startup clusters are weighted, so raw review summaries are sensitive to cluster imbalance.

RQ2: Topic Distribution and Satisfaction

rq2_df <- master_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(cluster), !is.na(topic_title), !is.na(sentiment), !is.na(sentiment_score)) %>%
  mutate(cluster = as.factor(cluster),
         topic_title = as.factor(topic_title))

cat("Rows used for RQ2:", nrow(rq2_df), "\n")
## Rows used for RQ2: 28234
cat("Clusters used:", n_distinct(rq2_df$cluster), "\n")
## Clusters used: 4
cat("Topics used:", n_distinct(rq2_df$topic_title), "\n")
## Topics used: 26
topic_cluster_counts <- rq2_df %>%
  count(cluster, topic_title) %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_total = sum(n),
    prop = n / cluster_total
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

print(topic_cluster_counts)
## # A tibble: 96 × 5
##    cluster topic_title                             n cluster_total     prop
##    <fct>   <fct>                               <int>         <int>    <dbl>
##  1 1       Account Management Issues             923         10547 0.0875  
##  2 1       Collaborative Analytical Platforms      3         10547 0.000284
##  3 1       Concerns Over AI Guidance               8         10547 0.000759
##  4 1       Concerns Over Subscription Services   116         10547 0.0110  
##  5 1       Consumer Complaints on Services       254         10547 0.0241  
##  6 1       Conversational AI Experience          111         10547 0.0105  
##  7 1       Customer Support Challenges            17         10547 0.00161 
##  8 1       Exceptional Customer Support          305         10547 0.0289  
##  9 1       Feedback on User Experience           166         10547 0.0157  
## 10 1       Privacy Concerns and User Rights      177         10547 0.0168  
## # ℹ 86 more rows
topic_cluster_table <- rq2_df %>%
  count(cluster, topic_title) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from = topic_title,
    values_from = n,
    values_fill = 0
  )

topic_cluster_matrix <- topic_cluster_table %>%
  select(-cluster) %>%
  as.matrix()

chi_topic_cluster <- chisq.test(topic_cluster_matrix)
chi_topic_cluster
## 
##  Pearson's Chi-squared test
## 
## data:  topic_cluster_matrix
## X-squared = 54604, df = 75, p-value < 2.2e-16
top_topics_by_cluster <- topic_cluster_counts %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = prop, n = 5, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

print(top_topics_by_cluster)
## # A tibble: 20 × 5
##    cluster topic_title                                    n cluster_total   prop
##    <fct>   <fct>                                      <int>         <int>  <dbl>
##  1 1       User Experience Issues                      2937         10547 0.278 
##  2 1       Subscription Service Concerns               2015         10547 0.191 
##  3 1       User Experience Feedback                    1379         10547 0.131 
##  4 1       User Experience Challenges                  1160         10547 0.110 
##  5 1       Account Management Issues                    923         10547 0.0875
##  6 2       User Experience and Pricing Issues          3491          8696 0.401 
##  7 2       Product Performance Issues                  1411          8696 0.162 
##  8 2       User Experience with Free Tiers              930          8696 0.107 
##  9 2       User Experience Feedback                     553          8696 0.0636
## 10 2       User Experience Challenges                   533          8696 0.0613
## 11 3       Collaborative Analytical Platforms           760          1435 0.530 
## 12 3       Python Script Troubleshooting                284          1435 0.198 
## 13 3       Concerns Over AI Guidance                    150          1435 0.105 
## 14 3       Topic 27                                     113          1435 0.0787
## 15 3       User Experience with Transcription Servic…    70          1435 0.0488
## 16 4       Conversational AI Experience                1797          7556 0.238 
## 17 4       Privacy Concerns and User Rights            1200          7556 0.159 
## 18 4       Feedback on User Experience                 1181          7556 0.156 
## 19 4       Topic 12                                     596          7556 0.0789
## 20 4       User Frustrations with AI Services           525          7556 0.0695
bootstrap_mean_ci <- function(x, B = 1000) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  vals <- numeric(B)

  for (b in 1:B) {
    vals[b] <- mean(sample(x, size = n, replace = TRUE))
  }

  tibble(
    mean_estimate = mean(vals),
    ci_lower = quantile(vals, 0.025),
    ci_upper = quantile(vals, 0.975)
  )
}

topic_satisfaction <- rq2_df %>%
  group_by(topic_title) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_satisfaction = mean(sentiment_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_satisfaction = sd(sentiment_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_satisfaction = sd_satisfaction / sqrt(n),
    positive_prop = mean(sentiment == "positive"),
    negative_prop = mean(sentiment == "negative"),
    neutral_prop = mean(sentiment == "neutral"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_satisfaction))

topic_satisfaction_boot <- rq2_df %>%
  group_by(topic_title) %>%
  summarise(
    out = list(bootstrap_mean_ci(sentiment_score, B = 1000)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(out)

topic_satisfaction_final <- topic_satisfaction %>%
  left_join(
    topic_satisfaction_boot %>%
      select(topic_title, mean_estimate, ci_lower, ci_upper),
    by = "topic_title"
  )

print(topic_satisfaction_final)
## # A tibble: 26 × 11
##    topic_title               n mean_satisfaction sd_satisfaction se_satisfaction
##    <fct>                 <int>             <dbl>           <dbl>           <dbl>
##  1 Collaborative Analyt…   777             0.943           0.299         0.0107 
##  2 Python Script Troubl…   301             0.924           0.352         0.0203 
##  3 User-Friendly Testin…   208             0.913           0.396         0.0274 
##  4 User Experience with…   541             0.900           0.405         0.0174 
##  5 Topic 27                165             0.879           0.466         0.0363 
##  6 User Experience and …  3581             0.841           0.528         0.00882
##  7 Concerns Over AI Gui…   168             0.833           0.544         0.0419 
##  8 Exceptional Customer…   502             0.695           0.681         0.0304 
##  9 Feedback on User Exp…  1380             0.654           0.740         0.0199 
## 10 Topic 26                243             0.556           0.793         0.0508 
## # ℹ 16 more rows
## # ℹ 6 more variables: positive_prop <dbl>, negative_prop <dbl>,
## #   neutral_prop <dbl>, mean_estimate <dbl>, ci_lower <dbl>, ci_upper <dbl>
ggplot(
  top_topics_by_cluster,
  aes(x = fct_reorder(topic_title, prop), y = prop, fill = cluster)
) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Top Topic Proportions Within Each Startup Cluster",
    x = "Topic",
    y = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(
  topic_satisfaction_final,
  aes(x = fct_reorder(topic_title, mean_satisfaction), y = mean_satisfaction)
) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Satisfaction by Topic",
    x = "Topic",
    y = "Mean Satisfaction Score"
  )

Conclusion

The chi-square test comparing topic distributions across startup clusters gives a p-value of <2e-16. The topic with the highest mean satisfaction score is Collaborative Analytical Platforms, while the topic with the lowest mean satisfaction score is Account Management Issues. Together, these results show that startup clusters differ in the kinds of review topics they receive, and that review topics also differ meaningfully in their associated satisfaction levels.

RQ3: Emotion Intensity vs Ratings

rq3_df <- master_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(arousal), !is.na(rating_original), !is.na(extreme_rating))

cat("Rows used for RQ3:", nrow(rq3_df), "\n")
## Rows used for RQ3: 32599
rq3_df_grouped <- rq3_df %>%
  mutate(
    rating_group = case_when(
      rating_original < 2 ~ "very low (< 2)",
      rating_original >= 2 & rating_original < 3 ~ "low [2, 3)",
      rating_original == 3 ~ "neutral (3)",
      rating_original > 3 & rating_original <= 4 ~ "high (3, 4]",
      rating_original > 4 ~ "very high (> 4)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    rating_group = factor(
      rating_group,
      levels = c(
        "very low (< 2)",
        "low [2, 3)",
        "neutral (3)",
        "high (3, 4]",
        "very high (> 4)"
      )
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(rating_group))
rating_arousal_summary <- rq3_df_grouped %>%
  group_by(rating_group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_arousal = mean(arousal, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_arousal = sd(arousal, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_arousal = sd_arousal / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(rating_arousal_summary)
## # A tibble: 5 × 5
##   rating_group        n mean_arousal sd_arousal se_arousal
##   <fct>           <int>        <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1 very low (< 2)   8779       0.0124      0.285    0.00304
## 2 low [2, 3)       1961      -0.0263      0.268    0.00606
## 3 neutral (3)      1923      -0.0623      0.238    0.00543
## 4 high (3, 4]      3491      -0.0747      0.198    0.00334
## 5 very high (> 4) 16445      -0.0273      0.229    0.00178
extreme_summary <- rq3_df %>%
  group_by(extreme_rating) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_arousal = mean(arousal, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_arousal = sd(arousal, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_arousal = sd_arousal / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    extreme_rating = ifelse(extreme_rating == 1, "extreme (1 or 5)", "non-extreme")
  )

print(extreme_summary)
## # A tibble: 2 × 5
##   extreme_rating       n mean_arousal sd_arousal se_arousal
##   <chr>            <int>        <dbl>      <dbl>      <dbl>
## 1 non-extreme       9038     -0.0634       0.217    0.00228
## 2 extreme (1 or 5) 23561     -0.00841      0.255    0.00166
bootstrap_mean_diff <- function(data, B = 1000) {
  vals <- numeric(B)
  n_rows <- nrow(data)

  for (b in 1:B) {
    boot_data <- data %>% slice_sample(n = n_rows, replace = TRUE)

    mean_extreme <- mean(boot_data$arousal[boot_data$extreme_rating == 1], na.rm = TRUE)
    mean_non_extreme <- mean(boot_data$arousal[boot_data$extreme_rating == 0], na.rm = TRUE)

    vals[b] <- mean_extreme - mean_non_extreme
  }

  tibble(
    mean_difference = mean(vals, na.rm = TRUE),
    ci_lower = quantile(vals, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    ci_upper = quantile(vals, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

set.seed(123)
diff_boot <- bootstrap_mean_diff(rq3_df, B = 1000)
print(diff_boot)
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   mean_difference ci_lower ci_upper
##             <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1          0.0551   0.0496   0.0610
t_test_result <- t.test(
  arousal ~ extreme_rating,
  data = rq3_df
)

t_test_result
## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  arousal by extreme_rating
## t = -19.493, df = 19128, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group 0 and group 1 is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.06057201 -0.04950372
## sample estimates:
## mean in group 0 mean in group 1 
##    -0.063444528    -0.008406666
ggplot(
  extreme_summary,
  aes(x = extreme_rating, y = mean_arousal, fill = extreme_rating)
) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Arousal: Extreme vs Non-Extreme Ratings",
    x = "Rating Group",
    y = "Mean Arousal"
  )

ggplot(
  rating_arousal_summary,
  aes(x = rating_group, y = mean_arousal)
) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = mean_arousal - 1.96 * se_arousal,
        ymax = mean_arousal + 1.96 * se_arousal),
    width = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Arousal by Rating Group",
    x = "Rating Group",
    y = "Mean Arousal"
  )

Conclusion

The mean arousal for extreme ratings (1 or 5) is -0.008, compared with -0.063 for non-extreme ratings. The bootstrap estimate of the mean difference (extreme minus non-extreme) is 0.055, with a 95% bootstrap confidence interval of [0.05, 0.061]. The two-sample t-test gives a p-value of <2e-16. Together, these results indicate a statistically supported difference in average arousal level between extreme and non-extreme ratings, although both means remain close to zero overall.

Save Useful Outputs

write_csv(master_df, "master_preprocessed_dataset.csv")

write_csv(rq1_df, "rq1_sampling_bias_dataset.csv")
write_csv(cluster_satisfaction, "rq1_cluster_satisfaction_summary.csv")
write_csv(comparison_df, "rq1_naive_vs_stratified.csv")
write_csv(bootstrap_summary, "rq1_bootstrap_summary.csv")

write_csv(rq2_df, "rq2_topic_distribution_satisfaction_dataset.csv")
write_csv(topic_cluster_counts, "rq2_topic_cluster_counts.csv")
write_csv(topic_satisfaction_final, "rq2_topic_satisfaction_summary.csv")
capture.output(chi_topic_cluster, file = "rq2_topic_cluster_chisq.txt")

write_csv(rq3_df, "rq3_emotion_intensity_ratings_dataset.csv")
write_csv(rating_arousal_summary, "rq3_rating_group_arousal_summary.csv")
write_csv(extreme_summary, "rq3_extreme_rating_summary.csv")
write_csv(diff_boot, "rq3_extreme_difference_bootstrap.csv")
capture.output(t_test_result, file = "rq3_t_test_result.txt")
